steps: Identify the greatest common factor of $9x^2$ and $5x$: both terms share a factor of x.
eliminationHtml:
• Choice A — '$x(9x + 5)$': Distributes to $9x^2 + 5x$, which matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — '$5x(9x + 1)$': Distributes to $45x^2 + 5x$, not $9x^2 + 5x$. ✗
  • Choice C — '$9x(x + 5)$': Distributes to $9x^2 + 45x$, not $9x^2 + 5x$. ✗
  • Choice D — '$x^2(9x + 5)$': Distributes to $9x^3 + 5x^2$, not $9x^2 + 5x$. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
